nuggets.headline: daft punk formed from a schoolboy rock band called darlin'
nuggets.text: Before they became Daft Punk, Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo were schoolboy friends in Paris who played in a rock band named Darlin', after a Beach Boys song. It was a negative review of Darlin' that described their music as "a daft punky thrash," which the duo found amusing enough to adopt as their new moniker.

source.title: Daft Punk: Homework Album Review | Pitchfork
source.quoteSnippet: schoolboy friends Bangalter and Homem-Christo’s rock band with future Phoenix guitarist Laurent Brancowitz, Darlin’—named after a track from the 1967 Beach Boys album Wild Honey that the three shared an affinity for—had disbanded... Melody Maker writer Dave Jennings notoriously referred to their songs as possessing “a daft punky thrash,” which led to the pair assuming the Daft Punk moniker.

nuggets.imageCaption: Daft Punk embraced their robot personas after their early band disbanded.

nuggets.headline: daft punk aimed to make a chic record without chic
nuggets.text: When crafting "Around the World" in Thomas Bangalter's childhood bedroom, Daft Punk had a clear vision: emulate the funk and elegance of disco legends Chic. Bangalter openly stated their goal was "making a Chic record with a talk box and just playing the bass on the synthesizer," because at the time, collaborating with Chic's Nile Rodgers wasn't an option.

source.title: Around the World (Daft Punk song)
source.quoteSnippet: Bangalter later described the process as "making a Chic record with a talk box and just playing the bass on the synthesizer," since they couldn't afford to collaborate with Rodgers at the time.

nuggets.imageCaption: Around the World by Daft Punk

nuggets.headline: daft punk's thomas bangalter helped launch fellow french house producer alan braxe
nuggets.text: If this is hitting right now, you need to hear Alan Braxe. Thomas Bangalter, one half of Daft Punk, helped champion Braxe's early career by releasing his debut single, "Vertigo," on Bangalter's own Roulé label in 1997. Braxe became a pivotal figure in the French house scene, known for his unique blend of filter disco and '80s pop.

source.title: Daft Punk - Wikipedia
source.quoteSnippet: Within a year, Braxe's early demos attracted the attention of Thomas Bangalter, and Braxe's debut single, "Vertigo", was released in 1997 via Bangalter's Roulé label.

nuggets.imageCaption: Alan Braxe was an early artist on Thomas Bangalter's label.

artistSummary: Daft Punk, the iconic French duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, rose from a schoolboy rock band to become pioneers of French house music. Known for their robot personas and genre-bending sound, they left an indelible mark on electronic dance music.
